capabilities: Ensure database schemas are decomposed to Third Normal Form (3NF) or Boyce-Codd Normal Form (BCNF) while leveraging knowledge graph structures for enhanced data relationships.

procedure.action: Run dependency analysis on schema using tools like SchemaSpy to identify functional dependencies using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Execute decomposition algorithm to convert schema to 3NF or BCNF, ensuring no loss of data integrity by executing the transformation function on each input record; assert output_schema_valid = true and error_count < 1; log transformation_duration_ms.

Invoke graph database tools like Neo4j to model and visualize relationships between decomposed entities by invoking the target API with the specified parameters; assert response_status < 400 and response_time_ms < 5000; log request_id and response_code.

Compose knowledge graph insights by mapping graph nodes to database tables, ensuring semantic consistency by composing module interfaces via adapter pattern; assert input/output type contracts match before connecting; assert integration_error_count < 1 after contract validation.
Assert schema design using query performance benchmarks, ensuring p95 query time is under 50ms.

Conduct peer review sessions to ensure schema aligns with both normalization and knowledge graph standards using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Integrate stepwise decomposition to 3NF or BCNF with knowledge graph insights to optimize database schema design, ensuring data integrity and efficient query performance.
title: Stepwise Decomposition to Third Normal Form with Knowledge Graphs

failureConditions: Data redundancy increases due to improper decomposition.
Query performance degrades due to complex joins.
Loss of data integrity during decomposition; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
Inconsistent data relationships across systems; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
metrics: input/output type contracts match before connecting
schema design using query performance benchmarks, ensuring p95 query time is under 50ms
successConditions: Check that all tables are in 3NF or BCNF using automated schema validation tools
Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on key queries to ensure no query exceeds 50ms p95 latency
Ensure all functional dependencies are documented and validated
Verify that knowledge graph mappings maintain semantic integrity
